inline_review:
commit 3bf71a81d752ad2c92119ee8d07e6a72695515fe
Author: Li Wang <liwang@redhat.com>

selftests/mm: skip hugetlb_dio tests when DIO alignment is incompatible

This commit adds get_dio_alignment() to query the filesystem's required DIO
alignment using statx. It then skips hugetlb_dio test cases where the write
size or buffer offset is not a multiple of the alignment, preventing
unexpected -EINVAL failures on filesystems with larger logical block sizes.

> diff --git a/tools/testing/selftests/mm/hugetlb_dio.c b/tools/testing/selftests/mm/hugetlb_dio.c
> index 9ac62eb4c97d..b125092fc6c1 100644
> --- a/tools/testing/selftests/mm/hugetlb_dio.c
> +++ b/tools/testing/selftests/mm/hugetlb_dio.c
[ ... ]
> @@ -33,6 +64,12 @@ void run_dio_using_hugetlb(unsigned int start_off, unsigned int end_off)
>  	const int mmap_prot  = PROT_READ | PROT_WRITE;
>  
>  	writesize = end_off - start_off;
> +	if (start_off % dio_align != 0 || writesize % dio_align != 0) {
> +		ksft_test_result_skip("DIO alignment (%u) incompatible with "
> +				"buf offset %u and writesize %zu\n",
> +				dio_align, start_off, writesize);
> +		return;
> +	}

Does start_off represent the memory buffer alignment rather than the file
offset alignment?

If it represents the memory buffer offset within the page-aligned hugepage,
should it be validated against stx_dio_mem_align instead of
stx_dio_offset_align?

Validating memory alignment against offset alignment might cause the test to
incorrectly skip valid cases or fail unexpectedly with -EINVAL if the memory
alignment requirements are stricter than the offset alignment.

[ ... ]
> @@ -89,37 +126,28 @@ void run_dio_using_hugetlb(unsigned int start_off, unsigned int end_off)
>  
>  int main(void)
>  {
> -	size_t pagesize = 0;
> -	int fd;
> +	size_t pagesize = psize();
> +	unsigned int dio_align = get_dio_alignment();
>  
>  	ksft_print_header();

Can get_dio_alignment() cause the TAP header to be skipped?

If get_dio_alignment() fails to open the temporary file, it calls
ksft_exit_skip() which immediately prints a skip message and exits. Since
this happens before ksft_print_header() is called, the required TAP version
13 header would never be printed.

Would this break automated test runner parsing by violating the TAP protocol
formatting?
output:
{"findings":[{"problem":"get_dio_alignment() can exit before ksft_print_header(), breaking TAP output formatting.","severity":"Medium","severity_explanation":"1. In `main()`, `get_dio_alignment()` is called during variable initialization, which executes before `ksft_print_header()`.
2. If `get_dio_alignment()` fails (e.g., if `open()` fails because `/tmp` does not support `O_TMPFILE | O_DIRECT`), it invokes `ksft_exit_skip()`.
3. `ksft_exit_skip()` immediately prints a skip message and exits the program. Because `ksft_print_header()` was bypassed, the required `TAP version 13` header is never printed. This violates the TAP protocol and can cause automated test runners to fail to parse the output."},{"problem":"The DIO offset alignment is incorrectly used to validate the memory buffer alignment instead of the DIO memory alignment.","severity":"Medium","severity_explanation":"1. The patch queries `stx.stx_dio_offset_align` and uses it as a single `dio_align` value to validate both the I/O length (`writesize % dio_align != 0`) and the memory buffer alignment (`start_off % dio_align != 0`).
2. According to the `statx` API contract, `stx_dio_offset_align` is the required alignment for file offsets and I/O lengths, whereas `stx_dio_mem_align` is the required alignment for user memory buffers.
3. Because `start_off` represents the offset within the page-aligned hugepage buffer, it dictates the memory buffer's alignment and must be validated against `stx_dio_mem_align`.
4. Using the offset alignment for memory alignment can cause the test to incorrectly skip valid cases (if offset alignment is stricter) or fail unexpectedly with `-EINVAL` during `write()` (if memory alignment is stricter)."}],"fixes":"","review_inline":"commit 3bf71a81d752ad2c92119ee8d07e6a72695515fe

[ ... ]
> @@ -89,37 +126,28 @@ void run_dio_using_hugetlb(unsigned int start_off, unsigned int end_off)
>  
>  int main(void)
>  {
> -	size_t pagesize = 0;
> -	int fd;
> +	size_t pagesize = psize();

>  
>  	ksft_print_header();

If get_dio_alignment() fails to open the temporary file, it calls

Would this break automated test runner parsing by violating the TAP protocol
formatting?
